india_trends.summary: India's oil refiners are prepared for changes in Russian crude supply. Weaker domestic demand and available oil from the US and Middle East offer alternatives. China's reduced market presence also helps maintain supply. Maintenance at major refiners has increased Russian crude in storage. This provi

india_trends.title: NSE EGR trading goes live. How can gold investors buy, sell and invest?
india_trends.summary: The National Stock Exchange of India (NSE) has launched live trading in its Electronic Gold Receipts (EGR) segment, enabling investors to trade gold digitally via demat accounts. Backed by physical gold stored in regulated vaults, EGRs allow flexible denominations and exchange-based trading. The ini

india_trends.title: US-Iran war: Why PM Modi visited UAE amid Middle East conflict and Strait of Hormuz crisis
india_trends.summary: Amid escalating tensions from the US-Iran war and soaring oil prices, India's Prime Minister Modi's recent visit to the UAE aims to secure energy supplies and foster deeper economic ties. With the UAE's strategic shift in oil policy, India seeks to diversify imports and enhance energy security.

india_trends.title: Why Apollo Tyres’ strong quarter may not mark a turning point
india_trends.summary: The management indicated that input costs could rise in the high-teens in Q1FY27, driven primarily by elevated natural rubber prices. Further, new vehicle purchases would be impacted if fuel/commodity costs continue their inflationary trajectory.

india_trends.summary: Narendra Modi has urged citizens to conserve fuel, avoid non-essential spending, and work from home where possible amid rising geopolitical tensions in West Asia. india_trends.title: Goldman says central banks want more gold for their reserves
india_trends.summary: In a bid to bolster reserves, central banks are poised to increase their gold purchases, potentially reviving gold prices as we approach the year's conclusion. india_trends.title: EXIM Bank eyes 10% jump in loan book in FY27 despite West Asia tensions
india_trends.summary: Export-Import Bank of India expects its loan book to grow 10% in FY27 despite geopolitical tensions in West Asia, according to Managing Director and CEO Harsha Bangari. The projected growth is lower than FY26’s 12% rise, which was partly boosted by favourable currency movements as a large share of t

india_trends.title: Why is Vodafone Idea share price falling despite a surprise ₹51,970-crore profit in Q4? Explained
india_trends.summary: Vodafone Idea share price opened at ₹12.81 today as against the last closing price of ₹12.96 and dropped to an intraday low of ₹12.45 soon thereafter, shedding 3.93%. The company had posted its Q4 results on Saturday.

india_trends.title: AI, not engineers for coding: Salesforce to spend $300 million on Anthropic tokens, CEO Marc Benioff says
india_trends.summary: Benioff's plans, coupled with his previous announcement that Salesforce will not hire any more engineers in 2025 due to productivity gains from AI, signal a decisive shift — the automation of writing code.

india_trends.summary: Indian stock markets are caught between global worries and strong domestic company profits. india_trends.title: NFP Sampoorna Foods IPO Day 1: Issue booked 20% so far. Check GMP, subscription status, key details to know
india_trends.summary: NFP Sampoorna Foods IPO opened on 18 May, priced between ₹52 to ₹55 per share. The company specialises in dry fruits, particularly cashew nuts, and aims to raise ₹24.53 crore through this offering. Subscription status shows 20% on day 1.

india_trends.title: Srinivasan Venu, Shruti Lohia-promoted firms to buy minority stakes in Jana Small Finance Bank
india_trends.summary: Jana Small Finance Bank is raising Rs 728 crore by selling equity shares, with Shruti Lohia's firm set to acquire around 4% and TVS Venu Group taking a 9.9% stake on a fully diluted basis. The bank is planning to upgrade to a universal bank, having reapplied for a license after an initial rejection.

india_trends.title: Govt weighs options to revive stalled IDBI Bank privatization
india_trends.summary: Indian authorities are exploring ways to revive the long-delayed sale of a majority stake in IDBI Bank after bids fell short of expectations earlier this year. Officials are considering lowering the reserve price by up to 20% and reducing reliance on the bank’s market price to make the deal more att

india_trends.title: Bharti Airtel claims No.2 spot: How it beat HDFC Bank to become India's second most valuable company
india_trends.summary: Bharti Airtel has overtaken HDFC Bank to become India’s second most valuable listed company. Airtel shares rose over 2%, taking its market capitalisation to around Rs 11.8 lakh crore, while HDFC Bank shares fell more than 2%, reducing its market cap to nearly Rs 11.7 lakh crore.
